title: Ensure that the --allow-privileged argument is set to false

input_row.title: Ensure that the --allow-privileged argument is set to false
input_row.rationale: The privileged container has all the system capabilities, and it also lifts all the limitations enforced by the device cgroup controller. In other words, the container can then do almost everything that the host can do. This flag exists to allow special use-cases, like running Docker within Docker and hence should be avoided for production workloads.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the master node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --allow-privileged argument is set to false .
input_row.remediation: Edit the /etc/kubernetes/config file on the master node and set the KUBE_ALLOW_PRIV parameter to "--allow-privileged=false" : KUBE_ALLOW_PRIV="--allow-privileged=false" Based on your system, restart the kube-apiserver service. For example: systemctl restart kube-apiserver.service 13 | P a g e Impact: You will not be able to run any privileged containers. Note: A number of components used by Kubernetes clusters currently make use of privileged containers (e.g. Container Network Interface plugins). Care should be taken in ensuring that the use of such plugins is minimized and in particular any use of privileged containers outside of the kube-system namespace should be scrutinized. Where possible, review the rights required by such plugins to determine if a more fine grained permission set can be applied.
input_row.default_value: By default, privileged containers are not allowed.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions running a command on the master node to check the `--allow-privileged` argument for the kube-apiserver. This can be verified through the Kubernetes API or using `kubectl` commands.
- The property can be queried via the Kubernetes API, specifically by checking the configuration of the kube-apiserver.
- The rationale and description indicate that this is a standard Kubernetes service property, which can be verified programmatically.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_allow_privileged_disabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API or `kubectl` to check the command-line arguments of the kube-apiserver.
- The command to use: `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].args}'`
- This command retrieves the arguments passed to the kube-apiserver container.
- The specific property to validate is that the `--allow-privileged` argument is set to `false`.
- **Pass Criteria:** The output should include `--allow-privileged=false`.
- **Fail Criteria:** The output does not include `--allow-privileged=false` or includes `--allow-privileged=true`.
